real_insight: When you have run every move your current framework can generate and the metric still has not moved, name the diagnostic limit precisely (list moves tried, name the limit, ask for a reframe) — vague despair masks the same signal that named-exhaustion would unlock.
objection: I went looking for 'when you've tried everything, just be vulnerable' — and the verse explicitly disclaims this. The falsifiability rules out premature giving-up: 'The verse describes Arjuna at the actual end of his case, not Arjuna giving up early.' The concrete scenario (eighteen months chasing a reliability score, named interventions: on-call rotation reorganized, Datadog instrumented, deploy pipeline replatformed through GitHub Actions, two principal engineers hired, weekly incident review, Sentry-based SLOs) is operationally specific enough that I recognize it as a real failure mode I have seen — the 'reorganize harder' loop where headcount and tooling additions never address the framing. The implication is precise: 'list the moves you have tried, name the diagnostic limit precisely, and ask for a reframe rather than another intervention.' This is a real escalation protocol, not wisdom theater. The 'directness unlocks the response that vague despair cannot' line names a specific failure mode of executive review communication that matters.
